LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANN BARRY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DECISION IN TEMPLETON DEVELOPMENT CORP. VS. SUPERIOR COURT (2006) 144 CAL APP 4TH 1073, CONTROLS IN THAT SEEKING MEDIATION IS NOT A CONDITION PRECEDENT TO FILING SUIT. THE AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES DOES NOT STATE THAT THE PROVISION IS A CONDITION PRECEDENT OR STATE A CONSEQUENCE FOR THE FAILURE TO COMPLY. GIVEN THAT DEFENDANT DRAFTED THE AGREEMENT, ANY AMBIGUITY MUST BE CONSTRUED AGAINST THE DRAFTER. LIKE ANY OTHER CONTRACTUAL PROVISION, THIS TERM MAY BE EXCUSED OR FOUND NOT TO BE MATERIAL. HERE, PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED AN ABILITY TO PLEAD FACTS THAT WOULD OBVIATE ANY ALLEGED HARM TO DEFENDANT BY THE DELAY, IF ANY, IN SEEKING MEDIATION UNDER THE HOLDING OF TEMPLETON. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jonathan Hughes of Howard Rice (765.4652) for defendant and moving party
John Bullock of Coblentz Patch (391.4800) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ANN BARRY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The breach of contract claim was timely filed based on the evidence presented. The 3 year statute of limitation, CCP 338, controls the negligent misrepresentation claim. Based on the evidence presented the gravamen of the complaint sounds in fraud. This in not a case of professional negligence as in Ventura County National Bank v. Marker (1996) 49 Cal. App 4th 1528, nor is Civil Code 2070 applicable for defendant is not a real estate broker. As a consequence, defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. On the issue of reliance, triable issues of fact are presented given the requirement that reasonable influences be construed in favor of plaintiff. The independent investigation by plaintiff does not preclude a finding of reliance. Bagdasarian v. Gragnon (1948) 31 C. 2nd 744, 748. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John T. Bullock of Coblentz Patch Duffy & Bass, 391-4800, appearing for plaintiff and responding party
Sarah M. King of Howard Rice Nemerovski, 434-1600, appearing for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ANN BARRY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled with 10 days to answer. The Court finds that the decision in Templeton Development Corp. vs. Superior Court (2006) 144 Cal App 4th 1073, controls in that seeking mediation is not a condition precedent to filing suit. The agreement between the parties does not state that the provision is a condition precedent or state a consequence for the failure to comply. Given that defendant drafted the agreement, any ambiguity must be construed against the drafter. Like any other contractual provision, this term may be excused or found not to be material. Here, plaintiff has demonstrated an ability to plead facts that would obviate any alleged harm to defendant by the delay, if any, in seeking mediation under the holding of Templeton. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
